summary:
This premature infant has signs suggestive of RDS; initial treatment includes CPAP or NCPAP but may require intubation and surfactant therapy based on severe tachypnea. \boxed{D} To address this case effectively requires prompt interventions such as CPAP/NCPAP followed potentially by ventilatory support including surfactants depending upon response and stability status. ```plaintext
This preterm infant shows signs consistent with Respiratory Distress Syndrome; start with CPAP/NCPAP, considering intubation and surfactant if not improving rapidly. 
``` Summary:

The baby appears to have RDS; initiate CPAP/NCPAP initially, readying options for intubation and surfactant if needed due to high rate of respiration. \boxed{D}
```

To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related directly to managing a neonate presenting these specific issues post-prematurity:

Premature infant showing RDS features—start with CPAP/NCPAP, prepare for possible intubation & surfactant use per worsening resp. status.\boxed{D}```
